domain_1_remembering_and_understanding.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas.

domain_2_applying.description: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

domain_3_analysing_evaluating_and_creati.description: Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.

general_rules: No chapter wise weightage is provided; care must be taken to cover all chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all sections.

class_xii_formative_topics: Surface Chemistry - Adsorption and Colloidal State
General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

class_xi_experiments: Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting, Bending, Drawing out glass tube; Boring cork)
Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
class_xii_experiments: Surface Chemistry (Preparation of sols, Dialysis, Emulsifying agents)
Chemical Kinetics (Effect of concentration/temperature on rate)
Thermochemistry (Enthalpy changes)
Electrochemistry (Variation of cell potential)
Chromatography
Preparation of Inorganic Compounds
Preparation of Organic Compounds
Tests for functional groups in organic compounds
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins
Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution
visually_challenged_experiments: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (Beaker, tripod stand, wire gauze, glass rod, funnel, filter paper, Bunsen burner, test tube, etc.)

class_xi_suggested_projects: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of methods of purification of water
Testing hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride etc. in drinking water
Investigation of foaming capacity of different washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate
Study acidity of tea leaves samples
Determination of rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids and bases on tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
class_xii_suggested_projects: Presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Quantity of casein present in milk samples
Preparation of soybean milk comparison with natural milk
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH/temperature
Comparative study of fermentation rate (wheat flour, gram flour, etc.)
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi
Study of common food adulterants

project_requirements.nature: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Fundamental Concepts and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Ability to understand the nature of matter, apply laws of chemical combination, perform stoichiometric calculations using mole concept, and handle measurement uncertainties.
competencies: Explain importance of Chemistry in career paths like medicine and engineering.
Distinguish between macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic representations of chemical phenomena.
Apply Dalton's Atomic Theory to explain atomic masses and percentage composition.
Perform stoichiometric calculations involving molar mass and mole concept.
Identify patterns in laws of chemical combination.

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Ability to trace the evolution of atomic models, understand quantum mechanical concepts, and relate electronic configuration to periodic properties.
competencies: Trace discoveries of sub-atomic particles (electron, proton, neutron).
Compare Bohr's model with the Quantum Mechanical Model of the atom.
Explain developments leading from classical models to modern quantum theory.
Relate electronic configuration to the structure of the periodic table.

competency_groups.title: Periodic Classification and Trends
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify elements based on periodic law, understand block types (s, p, d, f), and predict trends in physical properties.
competencies: Justify the need for classification of elements.
Apply Modern Periodic Law to explain element arrangement.
Determine electronic configurations for s-, p-, d-, and f-block elements.
Analyze periodic trends in atomic radii, ionization enthalpy, and electronegativity.
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Ability to apply various bonding theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, MO Theory) to predict molecular geometry and bond properties.
competencies: Differentiate between ionic, covalent, and coordinate bonds using Kossel-Lewis approach.
Predict molecular shapes using VSEPR theory based on electron pair repulsion.
Explain hybridization concepts (sp, sp2, sp3) in organic molecules.
Apply Molecular Orbital Theory to explain bonding in homonuclear diatomic molecules.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamic Principles
competency_groups.description: Ability to apply thermodynamic laws, calculate enthalpy changes, and determine spontaneity using Gibbs energy.
competencies: Define system types (open, closed, isolated) and state functions.
Calculate internal energy change ($\Delta U$) and enthalpy change ($\Delta H$) via calorimetry.
Distinguish between exothermic and endothermic reactions based on reaction enthalpies.
Predict spontaneity of a process using Gibbs Energy Change criteria.

competency_groups.title: Chemical and Ionic Equilibrium
competency_groups.description: Ability to analyze dynamic equilibrium, calculate equilibrium constants (K), and understand factors affecting ionic equilibria in solutions.
competencies: Distinguish between physical and chemical equilibrium concepts.
Apply Law of Chemical Equilibrium to derive expressions for Kc and Kp.
Relate Gibbs Energy ($\Delta G$) with Reaction Quotient (Q) and Equilibrium Constant (K).
Analyze factors affecting equilibria including Le Chatelier's principle applications.

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Ability to identify oxidation-reduction processes, assign oxidation numbers, and understand electrode reactions.
competencies: Define redox reactions in terms of electron transfer and classical concepts.
Assign correct oxidation numbers based on established rules.
Balance complex redox equations using the ion-electron method or oxidation number method.
Relate redox processes to electrode functions.

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms
competency_groups.description: Ability to name organic compounds, understand isomerism, classify functional groups, and propose reaction mechanisms.
competencies: Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules for various organic families.
Identify different types of structural and stereoisomers.
Classify carbon atoms (primary, secondary, tertiary) in complex structures.
Propose fundamental concepts behind reaction mechanisms including electrophiles and nucleophiles.
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Properties
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify hydrocarbons, understand their preparation methods, and evaluate environmental impacts like toxicity.
competencies: Differentiate between alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic compounds based on structure.
Explain the carcinogenic nature of certain hydrocarbons (e.g., benzene derivatives).
Discuss general properties distinguishing saturated from unsaturated hydrocarbons.

competency_groups.title: Solutions and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Ability to express solution concentrations, apply Raoult's Law, and determine molar masses using colligative properties.
competencies: Calculate concentration in various units (molarity, molality, mole fraction).
Explain deviations from ideal behavior in solutions.
Apply Colligative Properties to determine molecular mass of solutes.

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry and Energy Conversion
competency_groups.description: Ability to analyze galvanic and electrolytic cells, apply Nernst equation, and understand corrosion mechanisms.
competencies: Differentiate between Galvanic (voltaic) and Electrolytic cells.
Apply the Nernst Equation to calculate cell potential under non-standard conditions.
Explain principles of batteries, fuel cells, and industrial electrolysis.

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Rates
competency_groups.description: Ability to determine reaction orders, apply integrated rate laws, and explain temperature dependence of rates.
competencies: Define rate of reaction and factors influencing it (concentration, temperature).
Derive and use zero-order and first-order integrated rate equations.
Explain Collision Theory and the role of activation energy.

competency_groups.title: d-Block and f-Block Elements Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Ability to describe electronic configurations of transition elements, explain their variable oxidation states, and discuss lanthanoid/actinoid contraction.
competencies: Explain general properties of d-block elements (transition metals).
Discuss the chemistry of Lanthanoids and Actinoids including f-orbital filling.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 4: d and f Block Elements

competency_groups.title: Coordination Chemistry Principles
competency_groups.description: Ability to apply Werner's theory, name coordination compounds using IUPAC nomenclature, and explain bonding theories.
competencies: Apply Werner's Theory of Coordination Compounds to explain structure.

competency_groups.title: Organic Synthesis and Functional Group Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Ability to synthesize haloalkanes, alcohols, phenols, ethers, carbonyl compounds, and amines; understand their reactions.
competencies: Explain methods of preparation for Haloalkanes and Haloarenes.

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols, Ethers, Carbonyls, Amines & Biomolecules
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify organic functional groups, describe their preparation methods, and understand the structure of biomolecules.
competencies: Differentiate between alcohols, phenols, and ethers based on structure.

competency_groups.title: Problem Solving and Scientific Inquiry
competency_groups.description: Ability to integrate life skills with chemistry concepts, solve problems in real-life contexts (climate change, environment), and conduct scientific investigations.
competencies: Apply problem-solving skills to environmental issues like climate change.

domain_1_remembering_and_understanding.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas.

domain_2_applying.description: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

domain_3_analysing_evaluating_and_creati.description: Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.

general_rules: No chapter wise weightage is provided; care must be taken to cover all chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all sections.

class_xii_formative_topics: Surface Chemistry - Adsorption and Colloidal State
General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

class_xi_experiments: Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting, Bending, Drawing out glass tube; Boring cork)
Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
class_xii_experiments: Surface Chemistry (Preparation of sols, Dialysis, Emulsifying agents)
Chemical Kinetics (Effect of concentration/temperature on rate)
Thermochemistry (Enthalpy changes)
Electrochemistry (Variation of cell potential)
Chromatography
Preparation of Inorganic Compounds
Preparation of Organic Compounds
Tests for functional groups in organic compounds
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins
Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution
visually_challenged_experiments: Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (Beaker, tripod stand, wire gauze, glass rod, funnel, filter paper, Bunsen burner, test tube, etc.)

class_xi_suggested_projects: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of methods of purification of water
Testing hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride etc. in drinking water
Investigation of foaming capacity of different washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate
Study acidity of tea leaves samples
Determination of rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids and bases on tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
class_xii_suggested_projects: Presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Quantity of casein present in milk samples
Preparation of soybean milk comparison with natural milk
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH/temperature
Comparative study of fermentation rate (wheat flour, gram flour, etc.)
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi
Study of common food adulterants
